title: bytearray + [< >] literal
manager: A first-class array of bytes with its own literal syntax (`[< ... >]`), for machines that touch binary — protocol frames, hashes, encoded blobs — without smuggling bytes through strings and praying the encoding survives the round trip. Bytes are bytes, kept distinct from text, with a deliberate byte view that the string ops can cross into when you actually mean to.
cs: bytearray type + `[< >]` literal; distinct from string; byte-view interop with string ops (W6.57); pairs with the encoding builtins (W6.54) and the pinned hash (W6.55).
